label: Impeccable exposes high-value design verbs as first-class subcommands under one parent skill, not standalone skills
recommendation: Model sk-design task verbs as command-visible projections under the existing design family, with optional redirect pins only as a later ergonomics layer.

label: sk-design has the target verbs in aliases and references but no command-visible task layer
recommendation: Add task-command metadata for bolder, quieter, distill, harden, polish, delight, colorize, and typeset without adding new workflow modes.

label: All eight target verbs clear first-class command threshold, but require different strictness categories
recommendation: Use strictness metadata: single-axis, register-gated-transform, orchestrator, and moment-gated.

label: Impeccable's routing rules load a command reference on first-word match and also load it when intent clearly maps to a command
Impeccable live mode treats action verbs as behavior-bearing references with action-specific variant axes
A high-value design verb should be first-class when it names a repeatable user job, loads a distinct reference contract, has command-specific traps or preconditions, and maps to existing owner modes

direction: Create new standalone skills or new workflow modes for bolder/quieter/distill/harden/polish/delight/colorize/typeset
reason: The corpus explicitly prefers one parent skill with many subcommands and optional pins, while sk-design already has five owner modes capable of hosting the verbs.

focus: [D2-7/D2] granularity gap: which high-value task verbs deserve first-class commands vs buried references (corpus: impeccable-main)
